source: RE-TRAC: Recursive Trajectory Compression for Deep Search Agents
summary: ICML 2026 paper introducing cross-trajectory exploration via structured state snapshots (evidence, uncertainties, failures, future plans). Agents generate summaries after each failed trajectory to enable iterative reflection and globally-informed planning. Achieves 15-20% improvement over ReAct on BrowseComp.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — structured trajectory snapshots are directly applicable to AgentForge's AIBlocked recovery path. Formalises the handoff between a failed attempt and the next retry with semantically structured fields rather than raw execution logs.

source: TRACER: Trajectory Risk Aggregation for Critical Episodes in Agentic Reasoning
summary: ICML 2026 paper introducing a trajectory-level uncertainty metric combining content-aware surprisal, semantic/lexical repetition, and tool-grounded coherence gaps to detect when agent trajectories are heading toward failure. Improves AUROC by up to 37.1% over baselines.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — the coherence gap metric (how closely agent actions match stated objective) maps directly to plan-to-implementation drift detection before AIReview, a gap not covered by any existing AgentForge issue.

source: Scaling Test-Time Compute for Agentic Coding
summary: Paper demonstrating that allocating more compute to successive attempts (not just retrying with identical resources) measurably improves coding agent outcomes. Claude-4.5-Opus improved from 70.9% to 77.6% on SWE-Bench via compute scaling. Identifies Parallel-Distill-Refine for sequential scaling.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — directly applicable to AgentForge's remediation loop. Progressive resource escalation per cycle ordinal (timeout × multiplier, prompt verbosity) addresses the gap where each retry gets identical resources regardless of failure count.

source: PASTE: Pattern-Aware Speculative Tool Execution (Act While Thinking)
summary: March 2026 paper from Shanghai Jiao Tong University and Microsoft Research. Mines recurring tool-call patterns from execution traces to pre-execute likely next tools during LLM thinking time. 48.5% reduction in task completion time, 1.8x throughput improvement.
relevanceToAgentForge: Low — requires parallel execution capability which AgentForge explicitly does not support (single-agent-per-stage, sequential execution). Pattern predictability insight is conceptually interesting but not directly implementable.

summary: ACL 2026 paper introducing multi-graph episodic/semantic/causal/temporal memory for agents. Achieves 0.7 LoCoMo score (highest reported), 95% token reduction vs. full context, 1.47s query latency. Uses cross-graph traversal for multi-hop temporal questions.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — AgentForge has no cross-run agent memory, but multiple existing Linear issues (HID series) already cover cross-run memory with various approaches. Adding another memory-oriented issue would duplicate existing work.

source: Darwin Gödel Machine: Open-Ended Evolution of Self-Improving Agents
summary: May 2025 paper (updated March 2026) introducing a self-improving system that iteratively modifies its own code and empirically validates improvements via Darwinian evolution and open-endedness research. Maintains archive of generated coding agents.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — conceptually inspiring for agent self-improvement, but requires agents to modify orchestrator source code autonomously. Too architecturally ambitious for a 1-2 sprint implementation in AgentForge.

source: Group-Evolving Agents: Open-Ended Self-Improvement via Experience Sharing
summary: February 2026 paper treating a group of agents as the fundamental evolutionary unit, enabling explicit experience sharing and reuse. Achieves 71.0% on SWE-bench Verified vs. 56.7% baseline. Overcomes isolated evolutionary branch limitation.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — experience sharing across agent types (Claude Code + Codex) is interesting, but multiple existing AgentForge issues already address cross-run memory, skill distillation, and experience injection.

source: DARWIN: Dynamic Agentically Rewriting Self-Improving Network
summary: February 2026 paper on agents that dynamically rewrite their own prompts and configuration based on performance feedback. Agents autonomously improve their own instructions rather than external human annotation.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — existing Linear issue 'feat(agents): add run-outcome-driven stage prompt evolution with A/B variant tracking' already covers prompt self-improvement for AgentForge agents.

source: Dynamic Model Routing and Cascading for Efficient LLM Inference: A Survey
summary: March 2026 survey paper covering routing paradigms: query difficulty, uncertainty quantification, reinforcement learning, and cascading for LLM inference. Shows dynamic routing reduces cost while maintaining accuracy via lightweight complexity classifiers.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — AgentForge lacks dynamic model routing, but multiple existing Linear issues (feat(runtime): route stages to lightweight models, feat(orchestrator): add confidence-based dynamic model routing, etc.) already address this gap.

source: Microsoft Conductor: Deterministic Orchestration for Multi-Agent Workflows
summary: May 2026 open-source YAML-first CLI for composing agents and tools without SDK code. Zero-token orchestration using Jinja2 templates and expression evaluation. Includes MCP server integration, human oversight gates, and live DAG visualization.
relevanceToAgentForge: High (architectural validation) — Conductor's philosophy (YAML-defined deterministic workflows, token-free orchestration layer, MCP integration) directly validates AgentForge's design. Does not introduce a new capability gap but could inspire MCP tool integration.

summary: May 2026 LangGraph v1.2 release adding DeltaChannel for incremental state deltas (vs. full re-serialization), node-level control with error recovery handlers, and Event Streaming API v3 with content-block-centric protocol.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — AgentForge has no streaming/incremental output, but an existing Linear issue 'feat(runtime): stream incremental agent progress events to dashboard via SSE' already covers this capability gap.

summary: April 2025 paper on agents that autonomously edit themselves by exploiting compiler/test feedback, achieving 17-53% performance gains on SWE Bench Verified. Demonstrates verifiability constraint: self-improvement only works in domains with objective feedback.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — conceptually relevant to AgentForge's test/compiler feedback loops, but existing Linear issue 'feat(agents): add run-outcome-driven stage prompt evolution with A/B variant tracking' already addresses the self-improvement angle.

source: A-MEM: Agentic Memory for LLM Agents
summary: February 2026 paper introducing Zettelkasten-inspired dynamic memory structuring without predetermined memory operations. Agents dynamically refactor how they organize knowledge over time via interconnected note networks.
relevanceToAgentForge: Low — assumes persistent memory across runs. AgentForge resets between runs. Multiple existing issues cover cross-run memory from other angles.

source: AgentFactory: Self-Evolving Framework Through Executable Subagent Accumulation
summary: March 2026 paper on a self-evolving framework that accumulates and reuses executable sub-agent patterns. Achieves collective evolution via shared skills library with 88% improvement after 6 rounds.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — skill accumulation is relevant, but existing issues 'feat(agents): add failure-reflection loop to distill run outcomes into reusable agent skills' and similar already cover this pattern.

summary: Microsoft multi-agent system (stable April 2026 in Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0) with single Orchestrator directing 4 specialized sub-agents (Websurfer, FileSurfer, Coder, ComputerTerminal) in serial execution without parallel coordination.
relevanceToAgentForge: High (architectural validation) — Magentic-One's serial orchestrator + specialized agents pattern is nearly identical to AgentForge's design. Validates rather than enhances; no new capability gap identified.

summary: Karpathy's 2026 framework for structured agentic engineering, marking the end of 'vibe coding'. AutoResearch automates ML experiments via self-improving loops with objective verification gates (compiler, tests).
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — conceptually validates AgentForge's deterministic state machine approach and objective verification model. Does not introduce specific new capability gaps.

source: Graceful Degradation Patterns in AI Agent Systems (Zylos Research)
summary: February 2026 Zylos Research overview of graceful degradation in AI agent systems: four-level fallback chains, circuit breakers, retry with exponential backoff for transient failures (429, 5xx).
relevanceToAgentForge: High (gap confirmed) — AgentForge has no graceful degradation on subprocess failure. However, multiple existing Linear issues already address this: 'feat(runtime): add tiered graceful degradation', 'fix(runtime): implement circuit breaker and adaptive retry', etc.

source: AI Agent Observability 2026: Evals, Traces, Cost Guide
summary: 2026 overview of agent observability patterns: OpenTelemetry integration, tail-based sampling, multi-dimensional cost attribution, trajectory-based contrastive learning from failed runs.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — AgentForge lacks per-stage telemetry, but multiple existing issues cover this: 'feat(runtime): add per-stage execution telemetry', 'feat(orchestrator): persist per-stage execution telemetry', etc.
